# Python Stack Patterns

Idiomatic Python for **this** project: a LangGraph-orchestrated, Bedrock-backed, Aurora/pgvector release-to-content engine. Target language is Python; the surrounding app is TypeScript/Next.js. This skill is concept-first and points at canonical docs — read the live doc before relying on any specific API, because these libraries move fast.

## Pull current docs first

Fetch these before writing non-trivial code; do not trust memory for API shapes.

- LangGraph graph API — https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langgraph/graph-api
- LangGraph interrupts / human-in-the-loop — https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langgraph/interrupts
- LangGraph persistence (checkpointers) — https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langgraph/persistence
- Pydantic v2 — https://docs.pydantic.dev/latest/
- uv (packaging / envs) — https://docs.astral.sh/uv/
- Ruff (lint + format) — https://docs.astral.sh/ruff/
- pytest-asyncio — https://pypi.org/project/pytest-asyncio/ and AnyIO testing — https://anyio.readthedocs.io/en/stable/testing.html

## Core conventions for this repo

- **Packaging & runtime:** use **uv** as the single tool (replaces pip/poetry/pyenv/virtualenv). `uv init`, `uv add <pkg>`, `uv sync`, `uv run <cmd>`, `uv lock`. Pin the interpreter with `uv python pin`. Commit `uv.lock`. Run everything through `uv run` (including in GitHub Actions) so the locked env is used. Layout is `src/`, `tests/`, `db/migrations/` per project conventions.
- **Lint/format/type:** `ruff check` + `ruff format` (configured under `[tool.ruff]` in `pyproject.toml`; Ruff subsumes flake8/black/isort/pyupgrade). Type-check with **mypy**. These gates are enforced by the project's smoke-tester skill — code that doesn't pass ruff/mypy/pytest will be refused at mark-pass.
- **Typing:** annotate everything. Prefer modern builtins generics (`list[str]`, `dict[str, X]`, `X | None`) over `typing.List`/`Optional`. Use `Annotated` for metadata (LangGraph reducers, Pydantic field constraints).
- **Data models:** **Pydantic v2** for I/O boundaries, evidence/feature/claim contracts, and LLM-structured output; use plain `dataclasses` or `TypedDict` for hot internal state. App config via `pydantic-settings` (`BaseSettings`).
- **Errors & secrets:** raise specific exceptions, never swallow broadly. Secrets come from env / the harness secret broker — never hardcode AWS/Bedrock/GitHub/ElevenLabs creds. Network egress is allowlisted by the harness; expect denials for non-allowlisted hosts.

## LangGraph patterns (the orchestration core)

The spec uses four small graphs with three human approval gates. Key current API:

- **Build:** `from langgraph.graph import StateGraph, START, END`. Define a state schema (`TypedDict` recommended; Pydantic `BaseModel` when you need validation, at a perf cost). `builder.add_node(name, fn)`, `add_edge`, `add_conditional_edges(src, router, mapping)`. Compile with `builder.compile(checkpointer=...)`.
- **State merging:** fields default to last-write-wins (overwrite). For accumulation, annotate with a reducer: `Annotated[list, operator.add]` to concatenate, or `Annotated[list, add_messages]` (`from langgraph.graph.message import add_messages`) for chat history. Parallel/fan-out nodes that write the same key **must** use a reducer or they will conflict.
- **Control flow:** nodes return a partial-state dict, or a `Command(update={...}, goto="node")` (`from langgraph.types import Command`) to update state and route in one step.
- **Human gates (interrupts):** `from langgraph.types import interrupt, Command`. Call `value = interrupt(payload)` inside a node to pause; the JSON-serializable payload surfaces to the caller (under `result["__interrupt__"]` on `invoke`, or via stream interrupts). Resume by re-invoking with `Command(resume=<value>)` on the **same `thread_id`** — that value becomes the return of `interrupt()`. This is exactly the Gate #1/#2/#3 approve/edit/reject pattern; surface the payload to the Vercel dashboard and resume the same thread when the reviewer acts.
- **Persistence is mandatory for interrupts:** compile with a checkpointer. `InMemorySaver` for tests, `PostgresSaver` (package `langgraph-checkpoint-postgres`) against Aurora for real runs. Config is `{"configurable": {"thread_id": "..."}}`. Same thread_id resumes saved state; new id starts fresh.
- **Async:** use the `a*` methods (`ainvoke`/`astream`) and async nodes for I/O-bound work (Bedrock, Aurora, GitHub API). Don't mix a sync checkpointer into an async run — use the async checkpointer variants.

## Checklist

- [ ] Pulled the current LangGraph/Pydantic/uv docs above — not relying on memorized APIs.
- [ ] Dependencies added via `uv add`; `uv.lock` committed; commands run through `uv run`.
- [ ] `ruff check`, `ruff format --check`, and `mypy` pass cleanly.
- [ ] Full type annotations; modern generics; `Annotated` where metadata is needed.
- [ ] Pydantic v2 models for evidence/feature/claim/LLM-structured contracts.
- [ ] Every graph that uses `interrupt()` is compiled with a checkpointer and addressed by `thread_id`.
- [ ] Parallel-write state keys have reducers; no silent overwrites.
- [ ] No bare `try/except` around `interrupt()`; pre-interrupt side effects are idempotent.
- [ ] Secrets from env/broker; no network calls to non-allowlisted hosts.
- [ ] Tests written (pytest) with a single, explicit async backend.

## Gotchas

- **Never wrap `interrupt()` in try/except.** It pauses by raising an internal control-flow exception; a broad `except` swallows it and the gate silently never fires.
- **Nodes re-run from their start on resume.** Code *before* an `interrupt()` executes again when the graph resumes — any side effect there (DB insert, S3 write, Bedrock call) must be idempotent or it double-fires. Put non-idempotent work after the interrupt or guard it.
- **Interrupt matching is positional.** Multiple `interrupt()` calls in one node are matched by index on resume; conditionally skipping one shifts the mapping and resumes the wrong value. Keep call order stable.
- **No checkpointer ⇒ interrupts don't work.** Forgetting `compile(checkpointer=...)` makes the graph run straight through (or lose state on resume). All three approval gates depend on this.
- **Parallel fan-out nodes writing the same state key conflict** unless that key has a reducer (`operator.add`, `add_messages`, etc.). `content_generation_graph`'s `generate_artifacts_parallel` is the live risk here.
- **pytest-asyncio vs AnyIO auto-mode clash.** If both are installed, `anyio_mode = "auto"` fights pytest-asyncio's auto mode. Pick one backend; if using AnyIO, drop `asyncio_mode` from pytest config (let pytest-asyncio stay strict) and add an `anyio_backend` fixture returning `"asyncio"`.
- **Pydantic v1 muscle-memory breaks on v2:** it's `model_validate`/`model_dump`/`model_dump_json` (not `parse_obj`/`.dict()`/`.json()`), `field_validator`/`model_validator` (not `@validator`/`@root_validator`), `model_config = ConfigDict(...)` (not class `Config`), and settings moved to the separate `pydantic-settings` package.
- **Pydantic state in LangGraph costs performance and can fight reducers** — validation runs on every state update. Use `TypedDict` for hot graph state; reserve Pydantic for boundary contracts.
- **`uv run` is not optional in CI.** Invoking `python`/`pytest` directly in GitHub Actions bypasses the locked env and silently uses system packages — always prefix with `uv run`.
- **Blocking calls in async nodes stall the event loop.** Bedrock/Aurora/boto3 sync clients inside an `async def` node freeze the whole graph; use async clients or offload with `asyncio.to_thread`.
- **LangGraph docs moved** from `langchain-ai.github.io/langgraph` to `docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langgraph` — old URLs redirect and the API surface (e.g. `interrupt`/`Command` now under `langgraph.types`) has shifted; always check the current page.
